name: cross-border-stablecoin-register
description: An open, versioned register mapping how jurisdictions regulate stablecoins — clause by clause, across 15 dimensions and 12 jurisdictions — exposed as typed MCP tools over the committed dataset.json. No network, no synthesis: every tool filters and reshapes the published register, and every returned record carries its source, pinpoint, claim_class, evidence_tier and version.

cross-border-stablecoin-register.description: Reads ./dataset.json (no network). Use an absolute path to mcp_server.py in a real client config; if mcp is installed in a venv, set command to that venv's python.

tools.name: about
tools.summary: Register metadata: name, version, record count, license, DOI, and the verification rule.

tools.name: list_jurisdictions
tools.summary: List jurisdictions covered, with full name and record count.

tools.name: list_dimensions
tools.summary: List the 15 dimensions with descriptions and how many records each has.

tools.name: get_record
tools.summary: Return the full record (all fields including requirement_structured and interpretation_note) by its id.

tools.name: query
tools.summary: Typed filter over the register.

tools.name: compare_dimension
tools.summary: Compare all jurisdictions on a single dimension — the core differential view.

tools.name: jurisdiction_profile
tools.summary: Return all records for one jurisdiction, ordered by the dimension framework.

tools.name: search
tools.summary: Keyword search across jurisdiction, dimension, authority, requirement summary, source, and tags.

tools.name: get_corridor
tools.summary: Return corridor model(s) — what clears and what breaks at each regulatory boundary along a cross-border flow.

tools.name: coverage
tools.summary: Coverage matrix: for each jurisdiction × dimension, whether a record exists ('verified') or not ('planned').

tools.name: compatibility
tools.summary: Query the §5.14 pairwise compatibility matrix (all 66 jurisdiction pairs from the Architecture working paper).

tools.name: interaction_sets
tools.summary: The six constraint-interaction sets (A-F) from Architecture §2.9 — the constraint pairs through which joint binding generates composition problems, ea

tools.summary: The Architecture paper's architectural patterns: the PRC three-pattern typology (§3.3 — direct subsidiary licensing / partnership distribution / separ

tools.name: open_questions
tools.summary: The §7 open regulatory questions (7.1–7.5) whose resolution will determine which architectural options become operationally viable, each preserved wit

tools.name: explain_feasibility
tools.summary: Explain WHY a corridor falls in its feasibility class: the rule that fired (origin drag vs destination gate), the justifying node-record basis for eac

tools.name: verification_report
tools.summary: The verification queue: which records are confirmed against official primary text and which are still pending.

tools.name: verification_worklist
tools.summary: The primary-source verification worklist: for every still-unverified cell, exactly what is missing to reach the next evidence tier and the instrument/

tools.name: citable_law
tools.summary: The lawyer-citable subset: only records that are a proposition of law (claim_class=tier1_legal), currently in force (status=in_force), AND confirmed a

tools.name: event_calendar
tools.summary: The regulatory event calendar driving date-aware compose(): dated/contingent CHANGES IN LAW that move a jurisdiction's signal.

tools.name: corridor_timeline
tools.summary: The dated future of a directed corridor: today's class, the scheduled transitions that change it (with their effective dates), and any contingent tran

tools.name: constraint_substrate
tools.summary: The constraint substrate: each (jurisdiction × constraint C1–C8) as a structured POLE from a controlled vocabulary, citing the tier1_legal record(s) i

tools.name: compose_via_substrate
tools.summary: DERIVE a directed corridor's feasibility by composing the two jurisdictions' C1–C8 poles through the interaction-set rules — the deeper engine behind

tools.name: stakeholder_database
tools.summary: The Atlas §8 stakeholder catalogue: the actor personas (issuer, distributor, regulators, treasury, holder, ...), each with its lens, the C1–C8 constra

tools.name: profile_for
tools.summary: PROJECT a directed corridor onto a stakeholder persona (Atlas §8).

tools.name: edge_coverage
tools.summary: Edge-layer coverage: how many directed corridors carry a record.

tools.name: corridor_skeleton
tools.summary: Return the corridor record for a directed edge: the hand-authored RICH record if one exists, otherwise the COMPUTED SKELETON (derived feasibility clas

tools.name: verification_ledger
tools.summary: The external primary-source verification pass (v0.9.5) audit trail.

tools.name: events_by_kind
tools.summary: The regulatory event calendar grouped by trigger_kind — the paper's typology of triggers by the KIND OF CERTAINTY each carries (flagship §3), not mere

tools.name: convergence
tools.summary: The cross-jurisdiction yield-line convergence view (flagship §4.5): independent legislative systems converging on the same functional boundary around

tools.name: forward_view
tools.summary: The per-jurisdiction supervisor forward view (Atlas §4.4): the trigger register re-sorted by jurisdiction into pending events, inbound/outbound edges that reclassify (own-trigger vs counterpart-driven), and ranked counterpart exposure; asserts no new facts.

tools.name: reconciliation
tools.summary: The computed-vs-authored corridor reconciliation: for every undirected jurisdiction pair, the class the compose() engine derives from the signal table

tools.name: records
tools.summary: The evidence-axis record browser: filter the register along the axes that decide citability — claim_class (KIND: tier1_legal vs tier2_operational), ev

guardrails: Static/offline: the server reads the committed dataset.json and never fetches the network.
No synthesis: tools only filter and reshape published records; no facts are generated.
What-if is conditioning, not forecasting: compose_corridor(as_of=…), corridor_timeline and events_by_kind apply only the register's own scheduled/contingent changes in law, with no probabilities.
Citable-by-construction: citable_law() and records(citable_only=true) return only tier1_legal + in_force + resolution_text cells, each with an official source.url + pinpoint.
Provenance-clean derived views: convergence and the computed layer assert no new facts (asserts_new_facts=false) and are cross-checked against the authored layer via reconciliation().

provenance.source_of_truth: dataset.json (compiled by build.py from the YAML records + analysis/ layer)
provenance.note: This manifest's tool list is generated from mcp_server.py; connect via docs/CONNECT_MCP.md.
